source.title: Sustaining attention to simple tasks: A meta-analytic review of the neural mechanisms of vigilant attention

confidence_rationale: Large meta-analysis (100+ neuroimaging studies) establishing the neural architecture of vigilant attention. Shows right-lateralized frontoparietal network sustains vigilance, with thalamic and brainstem arousal circuits maintaining cortical activation. The 'high arousal + behavioral restraint' profile directly maps to MECH-026's ready vigilance mode. Confidence moderated by the gap between human vigilance-task paradigms and REE's computational sensitivity-without-action mode.
failure_signatures: The meta-analysis focuses on vigilance to simple detection tasks (signal detection paradigm), not on ethically-charged restraint. REE's ready vigilance involves moral sensitivity, not perceptual monitoring.
Vigilance decrement (declining performance over time) is a core finding -- REE's ready mode has no obvious analogue to temporal degradation, which may indicate the mapping is incomplete.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Vigilant attention recruits a right-lateralized frontoparietal network (dorsolateral PFC, anterior insula, inferior parietal lobule) plus thalamic and brainstem arousal systems. The neural state involves high cortical arousal with suppressed motor output -- the organism is maximally alert but behaviorally quiescent. Vigilance decrement correlates with declining right PFC and parietal activation over time.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-026 claims ready vigilance primes restraint under high sensitivity without action. The meta-analytic finding that vigilance = high arousal + suppressed motor output provides direct neural precedent: the brain has a dedicated mode for being maximally sensitive while producing no motor output. In REE, this maps to elevated E3 sensitivity (precision weighting) with suppressed action selection -- the system monitors but does not act. The right PFC role in behavioral inhibition during vigilance parallels E3's restraint function.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Vigilance in the psychological literature means sustained attention to rare signals in monotonous tasks. MECH-026's ready vigilance is about ethical sensitivity with motor restraint. The arousal/inhibition architecture may be shared, but the cognitive content (perceptual detection vs. ethical monitoring) differs substantially. The vigilance decrement phenomenon has no clear REE analogue and may represent a biological constraint that computational systems don't share.
mapping.source_context: Human neuroimaging meta-analysis across vigilance/sustained attention paradigms (CPT, psychomotor vigilance task, signal detection)

confidence_components.notes: Psychological Bulletin meta-analysis, high methodological quality. Mapping fidelity moderate: the arousal-without-action profile maps well but the cognitive content (perceptual vs ethical vigilance) differs. Transfer risk moderate: human vigilance paradigms to computational restraint mode.
